title: Turn-intent discrimination ahead of the yield decision (generic)
approach: Insert a classification step between VAD/ASR onset detection and the turn-taking controller's yield decision. Instead of a single words-to-interrupt or min-silence threshold deciding yield/hold for every caller utterance, the step classifies the utterance's floor intent (taking the floor vs. giving feedback) using signals beyond raw energy -- lexical content, prosody, and duration together -- and only a take-intent classification is forwarded to the controller as a floor-taking event. A feedback-intent utterance is logged but does not interrupt the agent's turn.
integration_points: sits between the VAD/ASR event stream and the turn-taking controller's yield decision
consumes the same caller-channel audio and partial transcript the controller already has, adds no new audio input
emits a floor_intent classification (take | feedback | none) per caller utterance onset, not a raw VAD boolean
the controller's existing interruption-sensitivity threshold stays in place as a pre-filter; this step only re-classifies events that already crossed it
